calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Vanda Russell'S Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Appointment Of A Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Oct-19-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The court notes the Order to Appear and Order to Show Cause issued by the court on Jul-28-2022 and orders additional sanctions of $500 each on Administrator Vanda Russell and Attorney Jessica Barsotti. The court closes the Estate for Lack of Assets, in open court.

All subject matters are taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lozada. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Appointment Of A Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Jul-27-2022 Continued To Oct-05-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Administrator (SFPA); India Ford, in court, Donald Ford, in court, and Lenette Russell, in court, in propia persona, Beneficiaries.

The court mentions the troubled history of this matter and summarizes the actions the court has taken to clarify the status of the Administration and to bring this matter to a close. Indiana Ford asks the court regarding her position. The court advises Ms. Ford regarding the history of Vanda Russell's tenure as Administrator and suggests that she seek the advice of an attorney if she intends to seek relief from the Administrator. Ms. Ford requests a continuance so that she can search for legal counsel. The court requests the appearance of the SFPA at the next calling of this matter.

The Status of Appointment of Successor Administrator, Status of Vanda Russell's Accounting, and Order to Show Cause issued Jun-22-2022, are continued to Oct-05-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An Order to Appear will be issued to Removed Administrator Vanda Russell and Attorney Jessica Barsotti to appear on Oct-05-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and for an Order to Show Cause as to why the court should not impose further sanctions of $500 each.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Appointment Of A Successor Administrator
Off Calendar Jun-15-2022 Continued To Jul-27-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA").

The court and counsel the statement filed on 6/01/2022 with the SFPA recommending that the court terminate the estate for lack of assets. The court points to the possibility of contempt proceedings wherein the estate would need to be represented by an administrator and that there is no confirmation that the estate has no assets. The court advises that it is inclined to continue this matter one additional time and issue an Order to Show Cause as to why this estate should not be terminated for lack of assets.

The matter is continued to Jul-27-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. An Order to Show Cause as to why this estate should not be terminated for lack of assets is set for the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Martinez. Not Reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-04-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Janet Boessenecker, via Zoom, for the San Francisco Public Administrator ("SFPA"), Interested Party.

Ms. Boessenecker summarizes the SFPA's position after having reviewed the court's register of actions and what it believes is the history and current status of the matter. The court advises that the court on its own motion can appoint the public administrator as successor administrator, with notice, which the court intends to do at the next calling of this matter. After additional discussion, the court confirms that neither the suspended Administrator nor her attorney of record appeared at today's hearing. The court confirms on the record that Wanda Davis is being removed as Administrator.

The matters on today's calendar are taken off calendar. A Hearing on Appointment of Successor Administrator is set for Jun-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court is to provide notice regarding the preceding.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Andam. Not Reported.

Off Calendar Mar-23-2022 Continued To May-04-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The court points to the previous two callings of this matter where there were no appearances by Administrator Vanda Russell and her attorney Jessica Barsotti which prompted the court to issue an Order to Appear on Jan-26-2022 and an Order to Show cause on Feb-23-2022. Sanctions are payable ten (10) calendar days from today and proof of payment of sanctions must be filed with the court by close of business on Apr-15-2022. The matters on today's calendar are continued to May-04-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Should there be no appearances on that date, the court shall notify the CA State Bar of the non-compliance.

MINI MINUTES: Tonia Richardson appeared via Zoom.
No other appearances.
Ms. Richardson is sworn and examined by the Court regarding funds totaling $47,494. Ms. Richardson testifies Vanda Russell was not a known relative and did not produce any identification proving otherwise.

The matter is taken under submission.

Petition To Determine Title To And Require Transfer Of Property To Estate And For Damages And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-27-2020 Continued To Mar-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Barsotti for Vanda Russell (Petitioner).

The Court noted that Ms. Richardson is not present. Ms. Barsotti stated that she has not heard anything from Ms. Richardson, that they received the Bank of America records and that she submitted them to the Court, but not timeley. Ms. Barsotti also stated that the accounts were closed on September 1, 2017 and that the subpoenaed records do not include statements for the date of close. The Petition to Determine Title to and Require Transfer of Property to Estate, etc. is continued to March 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Title To And Require Transfer Of Property To Estate And For Damages And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-09-2019 Continued To Jan-27-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Barsotti for Vanda Russell (Administrator).

Ms. Barsotti stated that she has not heard from Ms. Richardson, that they have not been able to get the Bank of America records ("B of A"), that they have talked with B of A, that they had an issue with the subpoena and that the bank has asked to give them until the 20th of this month. Ms. Barsotti also stated that she believes the B of A funds were admitted, that she received phone calls from a number of the Beneficiaries, that some said they received $4,000.00, that some said they didn't receive anything, that Ms. Richardson has not submitted anything and that she is concerned that the funds are not there anymore. The Court stated that it cannot move forward on the B of A account without proof, asks Counsel what her preference is and stated that if there is some urgency, it can go forward with the SF Fire Credit Union account. Ms. Barsotti stated that it is cleaner to do it all at once and that she prefers to continue the matter one more time. The Petition to Determine Title to and Require Transfer of Property to Estate, etc. is continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Title To And Require Transfer Of Property To Estate And For Damages And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Continued To Dec-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Jessica Barsotti for and with Vanda Russell.
Tonia Richardson, niece, in propria persona.

Ms. Richardson stated that she has not been able to find counsel, that she is suffering from hardship, that she was injured at work, and struggling to pay her mortgage. Ms. Barsotti stated that this matter was on calendar 30 days ago, that Ms. Richardson should have obtained an attorney or filed objections, that the petition is ready to be finalized, that she talked to different heirs who stated Ms. Richardson had given different amounts of money to different people. Ms. Barsotti further stated that at the last hearing they discussed the funds taken from the Bank of America account by Ms. Richardson via small affidavit, that her subpoena was rejected by Bank of America, and requested the court to issue an order for those records. The Court stated it will not issue an order without a petition and will continue this matter one more time only for Ms. Richardson to either file objections on her own or with a counsel. Ms. Richardson stated she does not know Vanda Russell to be a relative, and that the family have come to an agreement on how to distribute. The Petition to Determine Title to and to Require Transfer of Property to Estate and for Damages and Attorney Fees is continued to December 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to move forward.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Determine Title To And Require Transfer Of Property To Estate And For Damages And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-07-2019 Continued To Nov-04-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Barsotti for Petitioner.
Tonia Richardson in propria persona (relative, contesting)

Ms. Barsotti stated she filed a declaration last Friday stating that Vyaisha Moss is a granddaughter and not a beneficiary. Ms. Richardson stated that the family members met and came to an agreement to distribute their uncle's money, wants to contest the petition and Administrator who is not a legal family member, and that she spoke with Ms. Barsotti on September 29, 2019 telling her how the money was going to be distributed. Ms. Barsotti stated they attempted to distribute informally, her client paid the final expenses as well as the moving expenses, her client, Vanda Russell, collected $19,000.00 from one account, and Ms. Tonia Richardson collected $49,000.00 from other accounts. The Court inquired as to filings with respect to the Bank of America account and filing of written objections. Ms. Richardson stated she wanted to seek legal representation and requested a continuance of a few months due to her upcoming surgery.

The Petition to determine title to and to require transfer of property to estate and for damages and attorney fees is continued to November 4, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on Ms. Tonia Richardson to get legal counsel, filing with respect to Bank of America account, and filing a written objection.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
